prompt_replacements.old:
Within each answer_request, author answer_expression, then question_input_uses, then answer_population.
question_input_uses assigns each input used by this answer_request to exactly one semantic owner kind.
Create exactly one question_input_uses record for each fact-local input_ref.
Use GROUP_KEY exactly for concrete members of a specified_question_inputs group key; source_value and temporal_bucket group keys have no GROUP_KEY inputs.
GROUP_KEY inputs are not candidate-row predicates; create no EXPLICIT_USER_CONSTRAINT for them.
Use POPULATION_TESTS when the input is an operand of one or more EXPLICIT_USER_CONSTRAINT membership tests, and give that use a unique use_id.
Use COMPUTE_EXPRESSION when a formula_value is an operand in the requested computed_scalar expression.
Use RESULT_LIMIT when the input supplies answer_expression's requested result limit.
One population test may reference several POPULATION_TESTS use_ids, and several tests may reference the same use_id.
Each EXPLICIT_USER_CONSTRAINT lists every POPULATION_TESTS use_id it consumes in question_input_use_refs.
GROUP_KEY, COMPUTE_EXPRESSION, and RESULT_LIMIT inputs have no use_id and cannot be membership-test operands.
SUBJECT_IDENTITY, NORMAL_INSTANCE_GUARD, and RAW_RECORD_GUARD use an empty question_input_use_refs array.
prompt_replacements.new:
Within each answer_request, author answer_expression, then answer_population, then question_input_uses.
question_input_uses assigns each input used by this answer_request to exactly one semantic owner kind.
Create exactly one question_input_uses record for each fact-local input_ref.
Use GROUP_KEY exactly for concrete members of a specified_question_inputs group key; source_value and temporal_bucket group keys have no GROUP_KEY inputs.
A specified_question_inputs group key restricts the result-key domain to its GROUP_KEY inputs and groups by them; do not also create population predicates for those inputs.
Use POPULATION_TESTS when the input is an operand of one or more EXPLICIT_USER_CONSTRAINT membership tests, and list those test IDs in membership_test_ids.
Use COMPUTE_EXPRESSION when a formula_value is an operand in the requested computed_scalar expression.
Use RESULT_LIMIT when the input supplies answer_expression's requested result limit.
One POPULATION_TESTS input may supply several membership tests, and one test may consume several POPULATION_TESTS inputs.
GROUP_KEY, COMPUTE_EXPRESSION, and RESULT_LIMIT inputs have no membership_test_ids and cannot be membership-test operands.

prompt_replacements.old: When a time input constrains an answer_request, assign it one POPULATION_TESTS use and reference that use from the applicable EXPLICIT_USER_CONSTRAINT.
prompt_replacements.new: When a time input constrains an answer_request, assign its POPULATION_TESTS use to the applicable EXPLICIT_USER_CONSTRAINT through membership_test_ids.

prompt_replacements.old: Before returning a complete contract, verify that each copied question occurrence is declared once, every fact-local input appears once in question_input_uses, group-key and compute-expression inputs appear in no population test, and every POPULATION_TESTS use is referenced by at least one EXPLICIT_USER_CONSTRAINT.
prompt_replacements.new: Before returning a complete contract, verify that each copied question occurrence is declared once, every fact-local input appears once in question_input_uses, group-key and compute-expression inputs appear in no population test, and every POPULATION_TESTS use names at least one EXPLICIT_USER_CONSTRAINT.
